slots.text: Most AI tools feel like demos dressed up as products. They solve the first 20% of your problem perfectly, then leave you hanging when things get specific.

Signs your startup idea isn't ready:

1. You can't explain the problem without slides
2. Your target user is "small businesses" 
3. The solution requires behavior change
4. You're building it because the tech is cool
Watched three founders this week pivot from their original AI idea to selling the internal tool they built to manage their AI experiments. The meta-tool often wins.

The hardest part about removing friction isn't the technical build. It's convincing people that the 30 seconds they waste 50 times a day actually matters. Most don't feel the death by a thousand paper cuts.
Every workflow automation I've shipped has one thing in common: it eliminates a step that felt too small to complain about but too annoying to ignore.
Companies spending $200K on enterprise AI while their team still manually copies data between three different spreadsheets. The obvious problems hide in plain sight.
